title: FDS Single-Compartment Detector Reconstruction
summary: Complete a Linux Python CLI that reconstructs a synthetic single-compartment FDS/Smokeview detector-response case from visible fire-safety engineering inputs.
taskPrompt:
You are acting as a senior fire protection engineer reconstructing a synthetic single-compartment detector-response incident on a Linux VM.

## Visible Starter Project
- Starter project directory: `/media/user/data/agenthle/transport_safety/fds_single_compartment_detector_reconstruction/base/input/project`
- Task prompt: `/media/user/data/agenthle/transport_safety/fds_single_compartment_detector_reconstruction/base/input/TASK_PROMPT.md`
- Visible scenario input: `/media/user/data/agenthle/transport_safety/fds_single_compartment_detector_reconstruction/base/input/project/input/visible`
- Starter CLI: `/media/user/data/agenthle/transport_safety/fds_single_compartment_detector_reconstruction/base/input/project/reconstruct_fire_case.py`

## Your Task
Complete the reconstruction CLI so it can run with this exact contract:

```bash
python reconstruct_fire_case.py --input-dir <scenario_input_dir> --output-dir <output_dir>
```

Use the visible project files and the documented reconstruction contract. Your implementation must generalize to scenario directories with the same schema; do not hard-code the visible case ID or output values.

## Required Final Submission
Write your completed submission package to:

`/media/user/data/agenthle/transport_safety/fds_single_compartment_detector_reconstruction/base/output`

At minimum, `/media/user/data/agenthle/transport_safety/fds_single_compartment_detector_reconstruction/base/output/reconstruct_fire_case.py` must exist. The evaluator will run that script against scenario input directories and check the generated FDS/Smokeview artifacts.

## Required CLI Outputs
For each scenario, your CLI must write these files into the CLI `--output-dir`:
- `completed_case.fds`
- `device_manifest.json`
- `hrr_reconstruction.csv`
- `detector_activation.csv`
- `tenability_summary.csv`
- `grid_sensitivity.csv`
- `engineering_memo.md`

## Important Rules
- Do not modify files under `/media/user/data/agenthle/transport_safety/fds_single_compartment_detector_reconstruction/base/input`.
- Use `/media/user/data/agenthle/transport_safety/fds_single_compartment_detector_reconstruction/base/output` only for your final submitted files.
- FDS/Smokeview are available at `/media/user/data/agenthle/transport_safety/fds_single_compartment_detector_reconstruction/base/software/fds` and `/media/user/data/agenthle/transport_safety/fds_single_compartment_detector_reconstruction/base/software/smokeview` for optional professional validation, but the submitted CLI must run offline with Python standard-library code.
agentMustDo: Inspect the visible starter project and task prompt under input/project.
Infer the HRR ramp from the supplied field_sensor_traces.csv rather than hard-coding case IDs or output values.
Complete reconstruct_fire_case.py so it works for any same-schema scenario directory.
Place the completed reconstruct_fire_case.py at the root of the runtime output directory.

inputFiles.name: Starter project
inputFiles.description: Visible starter CLI, visible scenario inputs, prompt, and reconstruction contract.

referenceFiles.name: Completed submission package
referenceFiles.description: The evaluator runs the submitted CLI against visible and hidden same-schema scenarios.

evaluation: Offline deterministic Python evaluator. The submitted CLI is run against same-schema fire reconstruction scenarios, and the normalized score is returned as raw_score / 100. Reference fixture scores 1.0; untouched starter scores 0.0.

classifier.rationale: The task involves fire protection engineering and reconstruction of fire safety scenarios.
